contraventionDescription: Stopped in a restricted area outside of a school. This is shown by yellow zigzag lines. Contravention Code 48

sections.heading: Stopped in a restricted area outside of a school. This is shown by yellow zigzag lines. Contravention Code 48
sections.content: You may not stop there for any reason during the times shown on the signs.
sections.contentHtml: You may not stop there for any reason during the times shown on the signs.

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
Below we show diagrams of the signs and bay markings which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
We would not condone knowingly parking on school zig zags as this could be dangerous for the school children!
1. The restrictions are shown by yellow zigzag lines on the road and roadside signage. You are not allowed to stop during the times indicated not even to drop the kids off at school. (Please note the word STOP rather than WAIT).  This is for reasons of safety to children. However were the zigzag markings clearly visible? Were the number of zigzags each side of the words “School keep clear” the same – they are meant to be. Were there other road markings such as single or double yellow lines through the zigzags? If so the markings may not comply with the regulations and you may have been confused as a result. Road markings and signs must comply with the regulations in order for the restrictions to be enforceable. Check out the diagram below and the permitted variants.
2. There must be a sign stating “No Stopping” and have a blue shaded circle with a red outline and cross through it. It should also be within 3 car lengths (15 meters) of where you parked. The sign below is the only sign that can be used with the keep clear yellow zigzags unless special permission has been given be the department of transport. Also note the permitted variants.
3.) Remember that you can cite compassionate grounds in any representation to the local authority although they would need to be pretty good if you stopped on zig zag markings.
 
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
 
Below: Signs that should be in situ at a School zigzag. Please note the permitted variations and any sizes shown.
 

 
Below: There are also below a diagram of the zigzag markings which should be in place on the road. Again check the dimensions and the permitted variants.
Back to Parking
